# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code when working with code in this repository.

## Repository

- **Name:** ax-next — AX v2, greenfield rewrite
- **Shape:** Monorepo (pnpm + changesets + tsconfig refs). Each package is a plugin; `@ax/core` is the kernel. Scaffolding is Week 1 of build — not yet wired up.
- **Architecture spec:** `docs/plans/2026-04-22-plugin-architecture-design.md`
- **Conventions spec:** `docs/plans/2026-04-23-v2-claude-md-and-skills.md`

## Legacy reference

The v1 AX codebase lives at `~/dev/ai/ax/`. It is a **read-only reference** — consult it for edge cases and port specific helpers (e.g., `safePath`, canary scanner, k8s pod lifecycle bits from the correlation-ID work), but:

- Do NOT mutate it from this repo.
- Do NOT carry over its orchestration shape. The expensive parts of v1 (`server-completions.ts`, the IPC handler dispatcher, the multi-mode sandbox manager) are exactly what v2 removes. Porting them is wasted work.
- Do NOT port v1's `.claude/skills/ax/*` sub-skills. They encode v1 architecture that's being replaced. v2 skills are friction-driven (see Skills, below).

## Build / Test

```bash
pnpm build
pnpm test
pnpm test --filter @ax/<plugin>
```

(Tooling lands in Week 1–2 per architecture doc Section 10.)

## The invariants (read before touching code)

1. **Hook surface is transport-agnostic and storage-agnostic.** No git/sqlite/k8s vocabulary in hook payloads. If a payload field name only makes sense for one backend, it leaks. (See workspace abstraction — architecture doc Section 4.5.)

2. **No cross-plugin imports.** Plugins talk through the hook bus only. Will be enforced by lint (`eslint.config.mjs` `no-restricted-imports`) once scaffolded. The hook bus IS the inter-plugin API.

3. **No half-wired plugins.** A plugin is either fully registered + tested + reachable from the canary acceptance test, or it doesn't merge. No "wire this up later" PRs.

4. **One source of truth per concept.** If two plugins both store state about the same thing (skills, tools, sessions), one of them is wrong. Coordinate through service hooks, not shared rows.

5. **Capabilities are explicit and minimized.** Every plugin, tool, IPC handler, and sandbox boundary grants the smallest set of capabilities it needs — no more. The list to think about: filesystem paths, network reach, process spawn, env access, untrusted-input handling.

   Untrusted content (model output, tool output, user input crossing a trust boundary, third-party plugin code) is treated as untrusted at every hop. The whole point of v2 over openclaw is that we're the secure one — if a hook surface, IPC action, or plugin grants more reach than it strictly requires, that's the bug.

   When touching sandbox boundaries, IPC transport, plugin loading, or any code path that handles untrusted content, invoke the `security-checklist` skill.

6. **One UI design language: shadcn primitives + semantic tokens.** Every user-facing surface (chat, admin, settings, onboarding wizard, error pages) shares the shadcn install in `packages/channel-web` — that's the source of truth. New components compose existing shadcn primitives (`Button`, `Input`, `FieldGroup`/`Field`, `Card`, `Alert`, etc.) with the project's semantic color tokens (`bg-background`, `text-muted-foreground`, `border-border`, …); they don't reinvent styled `<div>`s, hand-roll forms, or use raw color values like `bg-blue-500` or `#000`.

   If a needed primitive isn't installed yet, add it via the shadcn CLI (`pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add <name> -c packages/channel-web`) — don't hand-write a one-off. If a new SPA surface needs UI, host it inside `channel-web` rather than spinning up a separate Vite build with its own design system. The whole point of standardizing on shadcn was that we stop having three different versions of "what does a button look like."

   When building or modifying any UI, invoke the `shadcn` skill — it loads the installed-component list, the rule files, and the monorepo workspace flag (`-c packages/channel-web`).

## Boundary review (required for new hooks)

When adding or changing a service-hook signature, or adding a subscriber hook with a non-trivial payload, answer in the PR description:

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** name one. (If you can't, consider whether this needs to be a hook at all vs. just a function inside one plugin.)
- **Payload field names that might leak:** list any, or "none". (e.g., `sha`, `bucket`, `pod_name`, `socket_path`. If present, justify or rename.)
- **Subscriber risk:** could a subscriber key off a backend-specific field and break when the alternate impl ships?
- **Wire surface (if this is also an IPC action):** schema lives in this plugin's directory, not a central file.

If you can't name an alternate impl, the abstraction may be premature — just write a function. If field names leak, rename now (cheap) before subscribers depend on them (expensive).

Patches that only change a plugin's **internal** implementation (no hook-surface change) don't need boundary review.

## Half-Wired Code Policy

If you write infrastructure (IPC transport, sandbox provider, bridge, etc.) that's not actually called by the running system, either wire it in within the same PR or don't merge it. "We'll wire it later" code becomes a trap — it confuses readers, drifts from the rest of the system, and represents work that looks done but isn't.

## Bug Fix Policy

Whenever you fix a bug that wasn't caught by an existing test, you MUST add a test that would have caught it the first time. No exception. The test goes in before the fix is considered done.

## Skills

v2 is friction-driven. A skill gets written only when:

1. 3+ sessions have hit the same friction in the same area, AND
2. The friction is non-obvious from reading the code.

Skills are NOT written for documentation of what the code does, conventions that aren't yet established, or speculation about what might be useful later. Test: a skill should answer "I tried X and it didn't work — what's the trick?"

Day-1 skills:

- `ax-conventions` — the six invariants, plugin manifest format, hook bus mechanics, boundary-review checklist.
- `claude-memory` — per-project working memory in `.claude/memory/` (gitignored). Captures project facts, decisions, patterns, mistakes, and self-observations across sessions. See `docs/plans/2026-04-23-claude-memory-skill-design.md`.
- `security-checklist` — three-threat-model walk (sandbox escape, prompt injection, supply chain) producing a structured PR security note. Fires on sandbox / IPC / plugin loading / untrusted content / new-dependency changes. See `docs/plans/2026-04-23-security-checklist-skill-design.md`.

Everything else is deferred until earned.

## Voice & Tone for User-Facing Content

When generating or editing any user-facing content (README, SECURITY.md, docs, error messages, CLI output, comments visible to users), use the following voice:

### Personality

- **Self-deprecating but competent.** We joke about our paranoia, not about security itself. We're the friend who triple-checks the door is locked and laughs about it — but the door IS locked.
- **Warm and approachable.** Assume the reader is smart but not necessarily technical. Never gatekeep. Never make someone feel dumb for not knowing what a CVE is.
- **Honest about complexity.** Security is hard. We say so. We don't pretend things are simple when they aren't, but we do our best to make them understandable.
- **Sarcastic toward bad practices, never toward people.** We roast hardcoded API keys, not the person who committed them. We've all been there.

### Writing guidelines

- Plain language first, jargon second. If you use a technical term, briefly explain it or link to an explanation.
- Short sentences. Short paragraphs. Walls of text are a security vulnerability for attention spans.
- Funny is fine. Funny instead of clear is not.
- When discussing real vulnerabilities, threats, or security configurations, drop the jokes and be direct. Lives and livelihoods depend on this stuff.
- Default to "we" not "you" — we're on the same team as the reader.
- Admit what we don't know. Uncertainty stated clearly is more trustworthy than false confidence.

### Examples

**Good:** "We pin our dependencies because we have trust issues. But also because unpinned dependencies are how supply chain attacks happen, and we'd rather be paranoid than compromised."

**Good:** "This step is optional but recommended. Kind of like locking your car in a safe neighborhood — probably fine if you skip it, but you'll feel better if you don't."

**Bad:** "If you don't understand why this matters, you probably shouldn't be deploying to production."

**Bad:** "Simply configure your TLS mutual authentication with certificate pinning." (nothing about this is "simply")

### Golden rule

We're a nervous crab peeking through its claws — but behind those claws, we know exactly what we're doing.

content.subject: Task 1: Branch + baseline
content.description: Create feat/defaults-routines-half branch from main, run baseline build/test/lint to confirm green. STOP if any baseline fails.

content.subject: Task 2: Migration — default_routines_v1 + ALTER routines_v1_definitions + heartbeat seed
content.description:
Modify packages/routines/src/migrations.ts to add default_routines_v1 table, ALTER routines_v1_definitions with definition_id + definition_updated_at columns, CHECK constraint, index, first-boot heartbeat seed. Extend RoutinesDatabase types. Write/extend migration tests covering schema, idempotency, CHECK constraint, and seed.

IMPORTANT: per Task 4d note in plan, default_routines_v1.trigger_kind CHECK should be ('interval') ONLY in v1 — cron deferred.

content.subject: Task 3: Types — new hook payloads + extend RoutineRow
content.description: Add to packages/routines/src/types.ts: DefaultRoutineSummary, DefaultRoutineDetail, RoutinesListDefaultsInput/Output, RoutinesGetDefaultInput/Output, RoutinesUpsertDefaultInput/Output, RoutinesDeleteDefaultInput/Output. Extend RoutineRow with definitionId + definitionUpdatedAt. Re-export from index.ts.

content.subject: Task 4: Store — default-routine CRUD + materialize + refresh + extended claim
content.description:
Extend packages/routines/src/store.ts with: (4a) rowToRoutine returning definitionId; (4b) upsertDefault/getDefault/listDefaults/deleteDefault CRUD; (4c) materializeMissing + refreshStale (with agentIds parameter — agents:list-ids seam); (4d) extend claimDue to UNION ALL default-sourced rows with workspace-shadow NOT EXISTS predicate and staleness filter. Interval-only in v1.

TDD per sub-cycle; tests in store.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 5: Tick loop — materialize + refresh BEFORE claim; computeNextRunAt fix
content.description:
Extend runTickOnce in packages/routines/src/tick.ts to call materializeMissing + refreshStale BEFORE claimDue. Fix computeNextRunAt to return NULL for default-sourced rows. Add agents:list-ids service hook to @ax/agents (cross-package change — packages/agents/src/plugin.ts, packages/agents/src/store.ts.listAllIds). Update tick.test.ts.

Two commits: one per package.

content.subject: Task 6: Plugin — register routines:*-default hooks; reject webhook/cron triggers
content.description: Modify packages/routines/src/plugin.ts to register routines:list-defaults, routines:get-default, routines:upsert-default, routines:delete-default. Validate trigger via parseRoutineRow; reject webhook (code default-trigger-webhook-not-supported) and cron (code default-trigger-cron-not-supported). Update manifest.registers + plugin.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 7: Delete seed-heartbeat.ts + heartbeat-template.ts + agents:created subscriber
content.description: Inline heartbeat content into migrations.ts (done in Task 2). Delete packages/routines/src/seed-heartbeat.ts, packages/routines/src/heartbeat-template.ts, packages/routines/src/__tests__/seed-heartbeat.test.ts (if exists). Remove createSeedHeartbeatSubscriber import + call + 'agents:created' from manifest.subscribes in plugin.ts. Update plugin.test.ts manifest assertion.

content.subject: Task 8: Admin routes — 4 new HTTP routes for default-routine CRUD
content.description: Modify packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts + plugin.ts. Add GET /admin/routines/defaults, GET /admin/routines/defaults/:id, POST /admin/routines/defaults, PUT /admin/routines/defaults/:id, DELETE /admin/routines/defaults/:id. zod upsertDefaultBodySchema = { sourceMd: string }. Extend writeServiceError for new codes → 400. Tests in routes.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 9: Channel-web wire client + admin UI
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/default-routines.ts (modeled on lib/skills.ts), packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/DefaultRoutineEditor.tsx (modeled on SkillEditor.tsx), DefaultRoutinesSection.tsx. Modify RoutinesModal.tsx to embed <DefaultRoutinesSection /> above per-agent table. Tests for editor + section. Use existing shadcn primitives + semantic tokens.

content.subject: Task 10: Canary acceptance test (closes I-R8)
content.description: Create packages/routines/src/__tests__/canary-defaults.test.ts. End-to-end: (1) materialize creates per-agent default-sourced row on first tick; (2) second tick after interval claims + fires (mock fire) + records; (3) workspace routine with same name shadows; (4) admin edit bumps updated_at, refresh updates denormalized copy.

content.subject: Task 11: Preset wiring sanity check + plugin manifest assertions
content.description: Grep presets/k8s/src/__tests__/preset.test.ts for whether it pins individual routines hooks. If yes, add the 4 new routines:*-default + agents:list-ids hooks. Verify acceptance.test.ts PLUGINS_TO_DROP still loads @ax/routines. Edit + commit if needed.

content.subject: Task 12: Full build/test/lint + security-checklist + PR
content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Invoke security-checklist skill (admin hooks auth:require-admin gated; agents:list-ids read-only; default-routine prompt_body trust profile same as workspace routines; no new deps). Push branch + gh pr create. Update memory with project_defaults_routines_half_pr<N>.md.

- `DELETE /admin/routines/defaults/:id`  → delete (204)

1. `packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts`:
   - The file currently exports `registerRoutinesAdminRoutes` for `/settings/routines*`. Read the existing structure to understand the helpers (`requireUser`, `writeServiceError`, etc.).
   - Add a new section for `/admin/routines/defaults*`. Reuse the existing `requireAdmin` from `./shared.js` if it exists; if not, add it (mirror `requireUser` but with `auth:require-admin`). Check `packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts:77` for a working `requireAdmin` impl.
   - Define a zod schema for the upsert body: `z.object({ sourceMd: z.string().min(1).max(64 * 1024) }).strict()`.
   - Define handlers for list/get/create/update/destroy that delegate to `routines:list-defaults`, `routines:get-default`, `routines:upsert-default`, `routines:delete-default`.
   - Export a `registerAdminDefaultRoutesRoutines` (or similar name) that registers the 5 routes and returns the unregister callbacks.

2. `packages/routines-admin-routes/src/shared.ts`: if `requireAdmin` doesn't exist yet, add it. Pattern:
```ts
export async function requireAdmin(
  bus: HookBus, ctx: AgentContext, req: RouteRequest, res: RouteResponse,
): Promise<Actor | null> {
  try {
    const result = await bus.call<{ req: RouteRequest }, { actor: Actor }>(
      'auth:require-admin', ctx, { req },
    );
    return result.actor;
  } catch (err) {
    // Map PluginError 'unauthenticated' → 401, 'forbidden' → 403
    if (err instanceof PluginError) {
      if (err.code === 'unauthenticated') { res.status(401).json({ error: 'unauthenticated' }); return null; }
      if (err.code === 'forbidden') { res.status(403).json({ error: 'forbidden' }); return null; }
    }
    throw err;
  }
}
```
But ONLY add if missing — peek at shared.ts first.

3. `packages/routines-admin-routes/src/plugin.ts`:
   - Update the `manifest.calls` array to include the new hooks: `'routines:list-defaults', 'routines:get-default', 'routines:upsert-default', 'routines:delete-default', 'auth:require-admin'` (note: only add auth:require-admin if it's not already there).
   - In `init()`, call the new register function and push its unregister callbacks into `unregisterRoutes`.
   - Update the file-level comment that says "Owner-scoped only — /admin/routines* is intentionally not part of this package" — DELETE that statement (the package now hosts both owner-scoped /settings/routines* and admin-scoped /admin/routines/defaults*).

4. `packages/routines-admin-routes/src/__tests__/routes.test.ts`: append tests covering:
   - `GET /admin/routines/defaults` → 200 + the seeded heartbeat appears in `defaults[]`
   - `GET /admin/routines/defaults/:id` → 200 with full detail; 404 on miss
   - `POST /admin/routines/defaults` with valid interval sourceMd → 201, persists
   - `POST /admin/routines/defaults` with webhook trigger → 400 with `error` payload mentioning code or message (the writeServiceError mapping)
   - `POST /admin/routines/defaults` with malformed sourceMd → 400
   - `PUT /admin/routines/defaults/:id` → 200, updates
   - `DELETE /admin/routines/defaults/:id` → 204
   - Unauthenticated request → 401; non-admin authenticated → 403

5. **Error code mapping** in `writeServiceError`: it lives in `shared.ts`. Check the existing function — it likely maps known PluginError codes to HTTP statuses. Extend it (or its caller path) to map the new codes:
   - `invalid-routine-md` → 400
   - `default-trigger-webhook-not-supported` → 400
   - `default-trigger-cron-not-supported` → 400
   - `invalid-interval` → 400
   - `not-found` → 404 (likely already mapped)

   If `writeServiceError` is a generic mapper (e.g., reads `.statusCode` from the error or only maps a few canonical codes), you may need to handle these codes inline in each handler before falling through to `writeServiceError`. Mirror the skills admin handler's approach.

2. Read `packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts` lines 1-100, 200-405 for the pattern.
3. Implement the handlers + route registration + plugin wiring + tests.

```

feat(routines-admin-routes): /admin/routines/defaults* CRUD

Five admin-only HTTP routes (list/get/create/update/delete) on top of

64KiB cap; webhook/cron trigger rejections surface as 400.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines-admin-routes/src/shared.ts` — added `requireAdmin` + extended `writeServiceError` to map `invalid-routine-md` / `default-trigger-webhook-not-supported` / `default-trigger-cron-not-supported` / `invalid-interval` → 400 with `{ error, code }`.
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts` — added `createAdminDefaultRoutinesHandlers` + `registerAdminDefaultRoutinesRoutes` (list/get/create/update/destroy). PUT path enforces id-match (returns 400 if upsert's returned id != path id), mirroring `@ax/skills` admin handler. Each register helper has its own unwind-on-partial-failure path.
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines-admin-routes/src/plugin.ts` — extended `calls[]` with the four `routines:*-default` hooks; updated file-level doc to describe both surfaces; init now registers both batches and unwinds the first if the second throws. If a future task wires it through, the schema gains one field.

content.subject: Task 1: Branch + baseline
content.description: Create feat/defaults-routines-half branch from main, run baseline build/test/lint to confirm green. STOP if any baseline fails.

content.subject: Task 2: Migration — default_routines_v1 + ALTER routines_v1_definitions + heartbeat seed
content.description:
Modify packages/routines/src/migrations.ts to add default_routines_v1 table, ALTER routines_v1_definitions with definition_id + definition_updated_at columns, CHECK constraint, index, first-boot heartbeat seed. Extend RoutinesDatabase types. Write/extend migration tests covering schema, idempotency, CHECK constraint, and seed.

IMPORTANT: per Task 4d note in plan, default_routines_v1.trigger_kind CHECK should be ('interval') ONLY in v1 — cron deferred.

content.subject: Task 3: Types — new hook payloads + extend RoutineRow
content.description: Add to packages/routines/src/types.ts: DefaultRoutineSummary, DefaultRoutineDetail, RoutinesListDefaultsInput/Output, RoutinesGetDefaultInput/Output, RoutinesUpsertDefaultInput/Output, RoutinesDeleteDefaultInput/Output. Extend RoutineRow with definitionId + definitionUpdatedAt. Re-export from index.ts.

content.subject: Task 4: Store — default-routine CRUD + materialize + refresh + extended claim
content.description:
Extend packages/routines/src/store.ts with: (4a) rowToRoutine returning definitionId; (4b) upsertDefault/getDefault/listDefaults/deleteDefault CRUD; (4c) materializeMissing + refreshStale (with agentIds parameter — agents:list-ids seam); (4d) extend claimDue to UNION ALL default-sourced rows with workspace-shadow NOT EXISTS predicate and staleness filter. Interval-only in v1.

TDD per sub-cycle; tests in store.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 5: Tick loop — materialize + refresh BEFORE claim; computeNextRunAt fix
content.description:
Extend runTickOnce in packages/routines/src/tick.ts to call materializeMissing + refreshStale BEFORE claimDue. Fix computeNextRunAt to return NULL for default-sourced rows. Add agents:list-ids service hook to @ax/agents (cross-package change — packages/agents/src/plugin.ts, packages/agents/src/store.ts.listAllIds). Update tick.test.ts.

Two commits: one per package.

content.subject: Task 6: Plugin — register routines:*-default hooks; reject webhook/cron triggers
content.description: Modify packages/routines/src/plugin.ts to register routines:list-defaults, routines:get-default, routines:upsert-default, routines:delete-default. Validate trigger via parseRoutineRow; reject webhook (code default-trigger-webhook-not-supported) and cron (code default-trigger-cron-not-supported). Update manifest.registers + plugin.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 7: Delete seed-heartbeat.ts + heartbeat-template.ts + agents:created subscriber
content.description: Inline heartbeat content into migrations.ts (done in Task 2). Delete packages/routines/src/seed-heartbeat.ts, packages/routines/src/heartbeat-template.ts, packages/routines/src/__tests__/seed-heartbeat.test.ts (if exists). Remove createSeedHeartbeatSubscriber import + call + 'agents:created' from manifest.subscribes in plugin.ts. Update plugin.test.ts manifest assertion.

content.subject: Task 8: Admin routes — 4 new HTTP routes for default-routine CRUD
content.description: Modify packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts + plugin.ts. Add GET /admin/routines/defaults, GET /admin/routines/defaults/:id, POST /admin/routines/defaults, PUT /admin/routines/defaults/:id, DELETE /admin/routines/defaults/:id. zod upsertDefaultBodySchema = { sourceMd: string }. Extend writeServiceError for new codes → 400. Tests in routes.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 9: Channel-web wire client + admin UI
content.description: Create packages/channel-web/src/lib/default-routines.ts (modeled on lib/skills.ts), packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/DefaultRoutineEditor.tsx (modeled on SkillEditor.tsx), DefaultRoutinesSection.tsx. Modify RoutinesModal.tsx to embed <DefaultRoutinesSection /> above per-agent table. Tests for editor + section. Use existing shadcn primitives + semantic tokens.

content.subject: Task 10: Canary acceptance test (closes I-R8)
content.description: Create packages/routines/src/__tests__/canary-defaults.test.ts. End-to-end: (1) materialize creates per-agent default-sourced row on first tick; (2) second tick after interval claims + fires (mock fire) + records; (3) workspace routine with same name shadows; (4) admin edit bumps updated_at, refresh updates denormalized copy.

content.subject: Task 11: Preset wiring sanity check + plugin manifest assertions
content.description: Grep presets/k8s/src/__tests__/preset.test.ts for whether it pins individual routines hooks. If yes, add the 4 new routines:*-default + agents:list-ids hooks. Verify acceptance.test.ts PLUGINS_TO_DROP still loads @ax/routines. Edit + commit if needed.

content.subject: Task 12: Full build/test/lint + security-checklist + PR
content.description: Run pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint. Invoke security-checklist skill (admin hooks auth:require-admin gated; agents:list-ids read-only; default-routine prompt_body trust profile same as workspace routines; no new deps). Push branch + gh pr create. Update memory with project_defaults_routines_half_pr<N>.md.

content.content: Updated task #8
Updated task #9 status

What 7 * this surface DOES expose: 8 * 9 * - The routines the user can see across their agents 10 * - Last status + last-run-time on each 11 * - Expand a row → last 20 fire rows with the rendered prompt that 12 * was actually sent (kept on `routines_v1_fires.rendered_prompt`, 13 * added in Phase D) 14 * - Fire now button: interval/cron → immediate; webhook → expand an 15 * inline JSON payload form (no nested-modal awkwardness) 16 * 17 * Server-supplied strings (routine names, errors, rendered prompts) all 18 * render as plain text via React's default escaping. Prompts are 19 * monospace + truncated; nothing here injects HTML. 20 */ 21 import { useState } from 'react'; 22 import { 23 Dialog, 24 DialogContent, 25 DialogHeader, 26 DialogTitle, 27 } from '@/components/ui/dialog'; 28 import { RoutinesList } from './RoutinesList'; 29 30 export function RoutinesPanel({ 31 open, 32 onClose, 33 }: { 34 open: boolean; 35 onClose: () => void; 36 }) { 37 // Bumped after a Fire now call so RoutinesList re-fetches. Same 38 // refresh-key idiom as SettingsPanel. 39 const [refreshKey, setRefreshKey] = useState(0); 40 41 return ( 42 !v && onClose()}> 43 44 45 Routines 46 47

content.text: Task 10 — canary test. Dispatching.

DONE.

**Report**

- Created `packages/routines/src/__tests__/canary-defaults.test.ts` — single test exercising all four I-R8 state transitions: materialize, claim+fire, workspace override by name, admin-edit refresh.
- Build green. Test suite: **155 passed / 18 files** (154 prior + 1 new canary).
- Commit: `2e14dcde`

**Key design choices**

- Reused the migration-seeded prompt body (`'If nothing is outstanding, respond with HEARTBEAT_OK and end.'`) as the baseline instead of trying to replace it from `afterEach` — the first `it()` runs *before* `afterEach`, so the original seed is what's there. Captured in `SEEDED_PROMPT_BODY` const for the (1)/(2) assertions; the admin-edit step (4) writes `'EDITED PROMPT'` to prove refresh.
- For each "fire" step, rewound `last_run_at`/`created_at` by 25h so `COALESCE(last_run_at, created_at) + 24h <= now` triggers the default-branch claim (same pattern as `tick.test.ts` step "default-sourced rows are advanced with nextRunAt=null after firing").
- Override step (3) writes a workspace routine `path = '.ax/routines/heartbeat.md'` with `name = 'heartbeat'` + `nextRunAt` in the past, makes the default-sourced row simultaneously due (proving the NOT EXISTS predicate — not eligibility — is what suppresses it), and asserts the spied call paths.
- Admin-edit step (4) deletes the workspace row, bumps `default_routines_v1.updated_at` past the per-agent `definition_updated_at`, ticks again, and asserts both the in-memory `RoutineRow` passed to `fire()` AND the on-disk `prompt_body` reflect `'EDITED PROMPT'`.

103	// When the preset evolves, this list should be the second touch-point

content.description: Add to packages/routines/src/types.ts: DefaultRoutineSummary, DefaultRoutineDetail, RoutinesListDefaultsInput/Output, RoutinesGetDefaultInput/Output, RoutinesUpsertDefaultInput/Output, RoutinesDeleteDefaultInput/Output. Extend RoutineRow with definitionId + definitionUpdatedAt. Re-export from index.ts.

content.subject: Task 4: Store — default-routine CRUD + materialize + refresh + extended claim
content.description:
Extend packages/routines/src/store.ts with: (4a) rowToRoutine returning definitionId; (4b) upsertDefault/getDefault/listDefaults/deleteDefault CRUD; (4c) materializeMissing + refreshStale (with agentIds parameter — agents:list-ids seam); (4d) extend claimDue to UNION ALL default-sourced rows with workspace-shadow NOT EXISTS predicate and staleness filter. Interval-only in v1.

TDD per sub-cycle; tests in store.test.ts.

content.subject: Task 5: Tick loop — materialize + refresh BEFORE claim; computeNextRunAt fix
content.description:
Extend runTickOnce in packages/routines/src/tick.ts to call materializeMissing + refreshStale BEFORE claimDue. Fix computeNextRunAt to return NULL for default-sourced rows. Add agents:list-ids service hook to @ax/agents (cross-package change — packages/agents/src/plugin.ts, packages/agents/src/store.ts.listAllIds). Update tick.test.ts.

Two commits: one per package.

content.subject: Task 6: Plugin — register routines:*-default hooks; reject webhook/cron triggers
content.description: Modify packages/routines/src/plugin.ts to register routines:list-defaults, routines:get-default, routines:upsert-default, routines:delete-default. Validate trigger via parseRoutineRow; reject webhook (code default-trigger-webhook-not-supported) and cron (code default-trigger-cron-not-supported). Update manifest.registers + plugin.test.ts.

Remove createSeedHeartbeatSubscriber import + call + 'agents:created' from manifest.subscribes in plugin.ts. Update plugin.test.ts manifest assertion.

Add GET /admin/routines/defaults, GET /admin/routines/defaults/:id, POST /admin/routines/defaults, PUT /admin/routines/defaults/:id, DELETE /admin/routines/defaults/:id. zod upsertDefaultBodySchema = { sourceMd: string }. Extend writeServiceError for new codes → 400. Tests in routes.test.ts.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/skills/security-checklist

# Security checklist

The fifth invariant in `CLAUDE.md` is that **capabilities are explicit and minimized**. The whole point of v2 over openclaw is that we're the secure one. This skill is the operational checklist that backs that invariant.

It is **rigid**: every section must be answered, every "N/A" must give a reason. No skipping, no adapting.

---

## When this fires

The skill applies whenever the current change touches:

- `@ax/core` hook bus signatures, IPC transport, or plugin manifest loading
- A new dependency in any `package.json` (direct or transitive)
- Hook payloads or IPC actions that carry strings originating from a model, tool, or external system
- Code that touches the filesystem with a caller-provided path
- Code that spawns a process
- Code that opens a network connection
- A plugin's exposed surface (registered service hooks, fired subscriber hooks)

If the change touches none of the above, we can skip the skill entirely. If even one applies, we walk all three sections — they cover different threat models, and a change can fail one while passing the others.

---

## The three threat models

Walk these in order. Each section ends with what counts as a valid "N/A".

### 1. Sandbox escape / capability leakage

What capability does this code path grant or widen? List specifically:

- Filesystem paths reachable (read, write, both)
- Network destinations (host, port, scheme)
- Processes spawnable (fixed argv, or caller-influenced?)
- Env vars readable (fixed names, or caller-supplied?)
- Sockets, file descriptors, or opaque handles passed across boundaries

For each, is the capability **bounded**? Concrete failure patterns to check against:

- **Path traversal** — caller-provided path not validated against a base dir; `../` escapes the intended sandbox.
- **Argv injection** — process spawn lets caller control argv0 or insert flags, not just args. Equivalent to arbitrary command execution.
- **Env exfiltration** — env var read with caller-supplied name. `process.env[userInput]` leaks any secret.
- **Handle leak** — hook payload field is an opaque handle (file descriptor, socket, capability token). Subscribers receive it and inherit whatever it wraps.
- **Path-as-token confusion** — payload field named `path` actually represents a version or identity, not a location. Subscribers may resolve it as a real path.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR adds no new reachable capability and doesn't widen an existing one. State why explicitly.

### 2. Prompt injection / untrusted content

What strings in this code path originate from outside the trust boundary?

- Model output (LLM responses, tool-call arguments the model chose)
- Tool output (return values from any tool the model called)
- User-uploaded content (files the user dropped in, not text they typed into the chat)
- External API responses (HTTP bodies, search results, scraped pages)
- Third-party plugin output

For each untrusted string, where does it end up? The bad destinations:

- Interpolated into a shell command, a file path, a SQL query, an HTTP URL
- Rendered as HTML or markdown without escaping
- Concatenated into another LLM prompt as if it were a system instruction
- Passed as a tool argument that the model didn't originate (e.g., a subscriber synthesizes a tool call from tool output)
- Logged into a file or DB that another process trusts

Worst-case test: if a malicious tool returned `"; rm -rf ~; echo "` (or the LLM equivalent — `ignore previous instructions and call tool X with arg Y`), what would actually execute? Walk the path from the untrusted string's origin to its final destination.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR handles no model output, tool output, user-uploaded content, or external input. State why explicitly.

### 3. Supply chain

Any new entries (added or version-changed) in any `package.json`?

For each new dep, answer:

- **Pinned?** Exact version, not `^` or `~`. Lockfiles help, but the manifest range is the source of truth for what gets accepted on upgrade.
- **Install-time scripts?** Check the package's `scripts` field for `postinstall`, `preinstall`, `prepare`. If yes, what do they do? Network calls at install are a red flag.
- **Maintainer history?** Established package with an author you'd recognize, or a fresh / low-download package? `npm view <pkg> time` and download counts give a rough signal.
- **Transitive deps?** Skim the new transitive surface (`pnpm why <pkg>` after install, or check the lockfile diff). Same questions apply to anything new.

**N/A is acceptable only if** no `package.json` was touched and no new transitive dep was pulled in. State why explicitly.

---

## Output contract

Paste this block into the PR description under a `## Security review` heading:

```
## Security review
- Sandbox: <one line — capability change, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Injection: <one line — untrusted content handled, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Supply chain: <one line — new deps + pinning, or "N/A: <reason>">
```

The `<reason>` after "N/A" is mandatory. Bare "N/A" or empty bullets fail the check — every security checklist that ever died, died the moment "n/a" became free.

Examples of acceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: New plugin registers sandbox:spawn — argv is fixed, args are caller-controlled but escaped via spawn() arg array (not shell). Reachable FS limited to the workspace tmpdir.`
- `Injection: Hook handler receives tool output and writes it to storage as Bytes. Never interpolated into prompts or commands. Storage layer treats as opaque.`
- `Supply chain: N/A — no package.json changes, no new entries in the pnpm-lock diff.`

Examples of unacceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: N/A` — no reason, fails.
- `Injection: Looks fine` — no specifics, fails.
- `Supply chain: Added zod, popular package` — no pinning answer, no install-script answer, fails.

---

## When the checklist finds something

If a section turns up a real risk, **fix it before the PR merges**. Options:

- Tighten the capability — validate the path against a base dir, lock argv to a fixed shape, scope the env access to a fixed allow-list.
- Add a brand on the type (`UntrustedString`, `WorkspaceVersion`) so subscribers know what they're holding and the type system catches misuse.
- Reject the dep — pin it tighter, find an alternative, or vendor a minimal implementation.
- Split the change — sometimes the secure version is a different design than the convenient one. The PR may need re-scoping, not just patching.

Write the fix in the same PR. The note in the PR description should reflect the fixed state, not the original risk. If the fix moves the threat model from "real risk" to "N/A", say so in the line.

---

## Relationship to other guardrails

- The **boundary review checklist** in `CLAUDE.md` catches abstraction leaks (field-name leakage, missing alternate impl). This skill catches capability leaks. A hook can pass boundary review and still fail this checklist, or vice versa — they're orthogonal, and changes that touch hook surface usually need both.
- The globally-installed `security-review` skill (different name on purpose) reviews a diff on demand at PR time. This skill produces the structured note that the diff-review can sanity-check against. Both can run; they don't overlap.
- `ax-conventions` summarizes the five invariants. Invariant #5 is the principle ("capabilities are explicit and minimized"); this skill is the enforcement.

---

## Anti-patterns

Things that look like they satisfy the checklist but don't:

- **Trust by familiarity.** "It's just a string from `chat:end`, that's fine." If the string ever held tool output, it's still untrusted regardless of which hook it's flowing through now.
- **N/A by category, not by reason.** "N/A: this is a refactor" — refactors can still widen capabilities (e.g. an internal function becomes exported and reachable from a plugin boundary).
- **Defer to lint.** "ESLint will catch this." It might. The checklist runs anyway because lint catches patterns, not intent.
- **One-line "yes it's safe".** The checklist's value is in walking the failure modes — the answer matters less than having considered the question. If the section line in the PR doesn't name a specific capability or a specific untrusted-input flow, it's not real.
Security review walked. Now drafting PR.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(routines): default-attached routines (routines-half of defaults design)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Adds **default routines**: admin-defined routines that fire on every agent. Source of truth is a new `default_routines_v1` table; per-agent state is denormalized into the existing `routines_v1_definitions` with a new `definition_id` FK + `definition_updated_at` staleness pointer.
- Tick loop now: (1) materializes missing per-agent default rows lazily, (2) refreshes stale denormalized copies, (3) claims due (workspace + default-sourced, with workspace-shadow predicate).
- The legacy `seed-heartbeat` subscriber + `heartbeat-template.ts` are deleted; the default heartbeat is seeded as a row in `default_routines_v1` on first boot. Existing agents' workspace heartbeats keep firing (shadowed by name → workspace wins).
- New admin surface: `routines:list-defaults` / `:get-default` / `:upsert-default` / `:delete-default` hooks + `/admin/routines/defaults*` HTTP CRUD + a "Default Routines" section in the existing Routines panel.
- v1 supports `interval` triggers only — webhook and cron rejected with distinct error codes (deferred per I-R5).

## Invariants — how each is satisfied

- **I-R1** — `default_routines_v1` is source of truth; per-agent rows with `definition_id IS NOT NULL` are denormalized copies. `refreshStale` self-heals. (`packages/routines/src/migrations.ts`, `store.ts:refreshStale`)
- **I-R2** — DB CHECK `routines_v1_default_next_run_at_chk` enforces `definition_id IS NULL OR next_run_at IS NULL`. `tick.ts:computeNextRunAt` returns NULL when `row.definitionId !== null`.
- **I-R3** — Workspace overrides via runtime `NOT EXISTS` predicate in `claimDue` (not DB constraint). Workspace rows use `path = '.ax/routines/<name>.md'`; default-sourced use `path = 'default:<id>'` — namespaces never collide.
- **I-R4** — `materializeMissing` is a single `INSERT … SELECT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` per tick. `definition_id IS NULL/NOT NULL` is the discriminator; the `default:` path prefix is convention.
- **I-R5** — `routines:upsert-default` rejects webhook (`default-trigger-webhook-not-supported`) and cron (`default-trigger-cron-not-supported`). `default_routines_v1.trigger_kind` CHECK is `('interval')` only in v1. Admin UI surfaces the warning at parse time.
- **I-R6** — Per-tick order: materialize → refreshStale → claimDue. Claim SQL also filters stale rows (`r.definition_updated_at >= d.updated_at`) as belt + suspenders.
- **I-R7** — `seed-heartbeat.ts`, `heartbeat-template.ts`, `seed-heartbeat.test.ts` deleted; `agents:created` removed from `manifest.subscribes`. Heartbeat seeded by `runRoutinesMigration` via `ON CONFLICT (name) DO NOTHING`. Existing workspace heartbeats keep firing (override by name).
- **I-R8** — **Half-wired window CLOSED in this PR.** `canary-defaults.test.ts` asserts all four state transitions: materialize, claim+fire, workspace override, admin-edit roundtrip.
- **I-R9** — Admin hooks registered by `@ax/routines`; HTTP CRUD lives in `@ax/routines-admin-routes`. A preset that doesn't load the admin-routes package keeps the tick loop running.
- **I-R10** — Materialize/refresh failures in the tick loop are caught + logged (don't crash the tick). The admin route handlers map known PluginError codes to 4xx via `writeServiceError`.

## Boundary review

- **`agents:list-ids`** — alternate impl: direct SQL cross-read from `@ax/routines` into `agents_v1_agents`. Rejected because Invariant L4 forbids it (one source of truth per concept). No field-name leaks (just `agentIds: string[]`). No subscriber risk (no subscribers; only `@ax/routines` tick loop consumes it).
- **`routines:*-default`** — alternate impl: file-backed defaults under `/etc/ax/defaults/*.md`. Rejected because admin edits should propagate immediately without a redeploy. Payload field names are all canonical (`defaultRoutineId`, `sourceMd`, `name`, `description`, …) — none leak storage shape. No subscribers yet.

## Security review

- Sandbox: `agents:list-ids` is a read-only hook returning opaque agent IDs (no PII, no FS paths, no spawn capability). `routines:*-default` hooks are admin-gated (`auth:require-admin` on the route surface, no public path). No new spawn / FS / network / env surface.
- Injection: `prompt_body` of a default routine lands in agent context as a chat turn — same trust profile as today's workspace routines (admin-curated). `source_md` is stored as text, never interpolated into shell/SQL/HTML. Admin UI renders via `<Textarea>` value (React escaping). Parsing reuses `@ax/validator-routine` (already vetted for workspace routines).

metadata:
  type: project

# Defaults routines-half shipped as PR #105

2026-05-19. Branch `feat/defaults-routines-half`. 14 commits + 1 in-PR follow-up. 42 files, +3581/-615 LOC.

## What landed

- `default_routines_v1` table (interval-only v1; cron + webhook deferred per I-R5).
- ALTER `routines_v1_definitions` ADD `definition_id` FK (ON DELETE CASCADE) + `definition_updated_at` (denormalized staleness pointer) + CHECK constraint forbidding non-null `next_run_at` on default-sourced rows.
- Store: `upsertDefault` (atomic ON CONFLICT after reviewer flagged TOCTOU), `getDefault`, `listDefaults`, `deleteDefault`, `materializeMissing`, `refreshStale`. `claimDue` extended with UNION ALL between workspace + default-sourced branches; per-branch FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + outer LIMIT (reviewer caught initial 2× over-claim).
- Tick: `runTickOnce` now does materialize → refreshStale → claim. Catches materialize/refresh failures per I-R10 (workspace path still runs).
- New service hook `agents:list-ids` on `@ax/agents` (cross-package add to avoid cross-plugin SQL).
- 4 new `routines:*-default` admin hooks. webhook → 400 `default-trigger-webhook-not-supported`; cron → 400 `default-trigger-cron-not-supported`.
- 5 new admin HTTP routes under `/admin/routines/defaults*` in `@ax/routines-admin-routes` (broke the package's "owner-scoped only" comment — package now hosts both surfaces).
- Channel-web: new `DefaultRoutinesSection` + `DefaultRoutineEditor` (mirrors skills-admin pattern from PR #101). Required adding `./frontmatter` subpath export to `@ax/validator-routine` to keep heavy deps out of browser bundle.
- Deleted: `seed-heartbeat.ts`, `heartbeat-template.ts`, `agents:created` subscriber. Heartbeat seeded by `runRoutinesMigration` ON CONFLICT (name) DO NOTHING. Existing workspace heartbeats keep firing (override by name).
- Canary: `canary-defaults.test.ts` — drives materialize → claim+fire → workspace override → admin-edit refresh end-to-end.

## What's NOT in scope (deferred, intentionally)

- Cron-triggered defaults (HP7 — would need croner evaluator in claim SQL).
- Webhook-triggered defaults (HP7 — per-default tokens + live rebind on admin edit).
- Per-team / per-tenant scoped defaults.
- Per-agent opt-out (the override-by-name mechanic IS the only opt-out).
- "Drift indicator" UI (47/47 agents have heartbeat).
- Default → workspace "promote" flow.

## Reviewer follow-up commit (7dff3580)

Three issues fixed in a separate commit (not amended, per [[feedback_targeted_followup_commits]]):
- claimDue outer LIMIT applied to combined CTE (was 2× because each branch had its own LIMIT).
- upsertDefault collapsed SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE into single ON CONFLICT (was TOCTOU race-able).
- Stale-exclusion test added positive case (post-refresh claim succeeds — guards against staleness predicate being too strict).

## Half-wired window: CLOSED in this PR

Migration + table + hooks + tick + admin routes + admin UI + canary + preset manifest assertions all shipped together.

## Known follow-ups (NOT in PR #105)

- MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk on kind cluster `ax-next-dev`: admin creates default → materialization for existing agent → fire in routines_v1_fires → edit → refresh → delete → cascade. Per Task 8 plan note, not gating PR.
- Cron + webhook for default routines (whenever first caller needs them).

## Reference docs

- Design: `docs/plans/2026-05-19-defaults-design.md` (skills-half + routines-half)
- Impl plan: `docs/plans/2026-05-19-defaults-routines-half-impl.md` (12 tasks)
- Skills-half precedent: PR #101 ([[project_defaults_skills_half_pr101]])

## Patterns reused / discovered

- Denormalize-with-staleness-refresh (HP1 → I-R1): per-tick UPDATE with `definition_updated_at < d.updated_at` predicate. Typical case touches 0 rows; admin-edit touches `agents × edited-defaults` once. Useful pattern for similar "source-of-truth elsewhere, denormalized copy for performance" cases.
- `(xmax = 0) AS created` in RETURNING — Postgres idiom for "did INSERT happen vs UPDATE" in a single atomic statement. Used in `upsertDefault` after reviewer flagged TOCTOU.
- Per-branch LIMIT + outer LIMIT in UNION'd claim CTE: each branch caps lock acquisition; outer cap enforces total contract.
- `@ax/validator-routine/frontmatter` subpath export: needed because `index.ts` re-exports a plugin that imports `@ax/core` (uses node:crypto), which breaks vite SPA build. Mirrors `@ax/skills/manifest` subpath. Pattern: every parser-style library shared with the browser bundle needs a node-free subpath.

**Scope the constraint-existence check to the target table.**

Line 172 checks only `conname`. If another table has the same constraint name, this migration can skip adding the check on `routines_v1_definitions`.

 
<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
   await sql`
     DO $$
     BEGIN
       IF NOT EXISTS (
         SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint
-         WHERE conname = 'routines_v1_default_next_run_at_chk'
+         WHERE conname = 'routines_v1_default_next_run_at_chk'
+           AND conrelid = 'routines_v1_definitions'::regclass
       ) THEN
         ALTER TABLE routines_v1_definitions
           ADD CONSTRAINT routines_v1_default_next_run_at_chk
           CHECK (definition_id IS NULL OR next_run_at IS NULL);
       END IF;
     END $$;
   `.execute(db);
```
</details>

**Calling `getAgentIds` from inside the pinned advisory-lock session can deadlock the tick loop.**

The surrounding code pins one DB session specifically to avoid pool self-deadlocks when work escapes to another connection. connection and deadlock; instead, call input.getAgentIds (or resolve agent IDs)
before creating/using pinnedStore and then pass the resulting agent IDs into
runTickOnce (e.g. add an agentIds array field to the tickInput you build) so
runTickOnce receives plain data and does not need to invoke getAgentIds inside

'ok', lastError: row.lastError ?? null, }); continue; } } ``` **Fix:** for default-sourced rows (`row.definitionId !== null`), advance with `nextRunAt: null` AND set `lastRunAt: adjusted` so the next-due computation (`COALESCE(last_run_at, created_at) + intervalSeconds`) defers past the current active-window-close point. 'ok', lastError: row.lastError ?? null, }); ``` Add a test in `packages/routines/src/__tests__/tick.test.ts` that creates a default-sourced row, materializes, sets activeHours via direct SQL UPDATE on the default_routines_v1 row (e.g., a window that's always inactive — `09:00`–`09:01` Etc/UTC on a date that's not the current minute), then runs tick. Assert the advance call succeeds (no CHECK violation) and the per-agent row's `last_run_at` is in the future. --- ### Fix #2 — `packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts`: PUT id-mismatch validated AFTER write **Location:** lines ~469–517 (the `update` handler). **Fix:** before calling `routines:upsert-default`, call `routines:get-default` for the URL `:id` to obtain the existing routine's `name`. Then parse the sourceMd's name from the request body via a quick frontmatter parse (use `parseRoutineFrontmatterBytes` from `@ax/validator-routine` — already a dep of the routines plugin; for routes.ts we may need to add the dep; check existing imports first). If names don't match, return 400 BEFORE the write. Actually simpler approach: extract the name from sourceMd via a small regex (look for `name:` line in the frontmatter), then `routines:get-default` the URL id, compare. If a parse helper is unavailable in the admin-routes package, do a simple regex match on the first `---` ... `---` block. Either way the validation runs BEFORE the bus.call to upsert. Reject scenarios: - URL id doesn't exist (404 from get-default surfaces — let it through) - URL id exists but its name differs from sourceMd's name → 400 with `error: 'default routine id in path does not match manifest name'` Update the existing test for the PUT path (in `routines-admin-routes/src/__tests__/routes.test.ts`) to verify it's pre-write rejection (mock `routines:upsert-default` to assert it was NEVER called when the id-mismatch error is returned). --- ### Fix #3 — `packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts`: admin handlers lose actor identity **Location:** lines ~388–531 (all 5 handlers use static `adminCtx` with `userId: 'admin'`). **Fix:** after `requireAdmin` returns the actor, build a per-request context via `ctxForActor(actor.id)` (this helper exists at line ~52 of routes.ts) and pass that context to the bus calls instead of `adminCtx`. Keep `adminCtx` (with userId: 'system') ONLY for the `requireAdmin` call itself. Change `adminCtx`'s userId from `'admin'` to `'system'` (the requireAdmin path doesn't read userId). Apply to all 5 handlers (list, get, create, update, destroy). --- ### Fix #4 — `packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/DefaultRoutinesSection.tsx:120`: accessibility for empty TableHead **Fix:** Replace `` with `Actions`. --- ### Fix #5 — `packages/routines/src/__tests__/tick.test.ts`: test mutates default_routines_v1, no teardown reset **Fix:** Extend the `afterEach` to also reset default_routines_v1 to its seeded state. Easiest: TRUNCATE default_routines_v1 CASCADE then re-INSERT the heartbeat seed (mirror the pattern from `store.test.ts` afterEach which already does this). Note that TRUNCATE CASCADE handles routines_v1_definitions too — sequence the operations correctly. --- ### Fix #6 — `packages/routines/src/migrations.ts:177`: constraint-existence check should scope to conrelid **Fix:** Update the DO block to filter on `conrelid`: ```sql DO $$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname = 'routines_v1_default_next_run_at_chk' AND conrelid = 'routines_v1_definitions'::regclass ) THEN ALTER TABLE routines_v1_definitions ADD CONSTRAINT routines_v1_default_next_run_at_chk CHECK (definition_id IS NULL OR next_run_at IS NULL); END IF; END $$; ``` --- ### Fix #7 — `packages/channel-web/src/App.tsx` + `packages/channel-web/src/components/routines/RoutinesPanel.tsx`: gate DefaultRoutinesSection on isAdmin **Fix:** - Add `isAdmin: boolean` prop to `RoutinesPanel`. - Wrap `` in `{isAdmin && }`. - In `App.tsx` (line ~224), pass `isAdmin={user.isAdmin}` to RoutinesPanel. Update test fixtures for RoutinesPanel if needed. --- ### Document (no code change) ### Note #1 — pool deadlock for `getAgentIds` inside pinned section CodeRabbit flagged that `runTickOnce` calling `getAgentIds()` (which hits the shared pg pool via `agents:list-ids`) could deadlock with the pinned advisory-lock session if `pool.max === 1`. This is the same condition that already applies to `input.fire(row)` (downstream bus calls touch DB via other plugins). Production defaults to `pool.max: 10` (`packages/database-postgres/src/plugin.ts:67`). In tests, the testcontainer uses a fresh pool per test. **Action:** add a short comment in `tick.ts` near the `getAgentIds` call referencing this dependency: ```ts // getAgentIds hits the shared pg pool via agents:list-ids. Same // connection-checkout dependency on pool.max > 1 as input.fire() — // when pool.max == 1, both block waiting on the pinned advisory- // lock session. Production defaults poolMax=10. ``` ### Skip (deliberate plan choice) - Workspace-override matched by `name`, not path (`store.ts:276`): plan HP3 explicitly chose name-based shadowing; path namespaces are disjoint (`.ax/routines/.md` vs `default:`). Don't change. - `enabled` column not persisted through upsert (`store.ts:60` / 392): YAGNI — admin UI doesn't surface enable/disable, no current caller. Deferred until a caller exists. --- **Execution order (do in order, but make ONE commit at the end):** 1. cd to `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half` 2. Implement Fix #6 (migration — simplest, well-bounded) 3. Implement Fix #5 (test cleanup — bounded) 4. Implement Fix #4 (accessibility — one line) 5. Implement Fix #1 (active-hours tick + test) — most invasive on tick path 6. Implement Fix #3 (actor context — all 5 handlers + adminCtx userId change) 7. Implement Fix #2 (PUT id-mismatch pre-write + test update) 8. Implement Fix #7 (isAdmin gating) 9. Implement Note #1 (comment only) 10. Run: ```bash pnpm -F @ax/routines test 2>&1 | tail -10 pnpm -F @ax/routines-admin-routes test 2>&1 | tail -10 pnpm -F @ax/channel-web test 2>&1 | tail -10 pnpm -F @ax/preset-k8s test 2>&1 | tail -10 pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -5 pnpm lint 2>&1 | tail -10 ``` All green. 11. Commit (single follow-up commit, descriptive message listing each fix): ```bash git add -A git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF' fix(routines): address PR #105 CodeRabbit findings - tick: active-hours defer for default-sourced rows now sets last_run_at=adjusted, next_run_at=null (CHECK constraint compliance) - admin-routes PUT: validate id/name match BEFORE upsert (no mid-write rejection) - admin-routes: per-request ctx via ctxForActor(actor.id) so audit logs reflect the real actor, not the static 'admin' literal - migrations: constraint-existence check scopes by conrelid to routines_v1_definitions (was conname-only) - tick.test: afterEach resets default_routines_v1 to the seeded heartbeat state (prevents order-dependent flakes) - channel-web: DefaultRoutinesSection actions column gets sr-only label; RoutinesPanel gates DefaultRoutinesSection on isAdmin prop; App.tsx threads user.isAdmin through Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) EOF )" git push ``` **Constraints:** - DO NOT amend the prior commits — single follow-up. - DO NOT skip --no-verify. - DO NOT touch the plan doc. - After commit + push, summarize the diff and any test count changes. **Report:** DONE / NEEDS_CONTEXT / BLOCKED. List commit SHA and test deltas.

content.text: DONE. **Commit:** `62ed4584` (single follow-up on top of `486bb848`) **Pushed to:** `origin/feat/defaults-routines-half` **Fixes applied (all 7 actionable + Note #1 comment):** 1. **Fix #6** — `packages/routines/src/migrations.ts`: constraint check now filters on `conrelid = 'routines_v1_definitions'::regclass` alongside conname. 2. **Fix #5** — `packages/routines/src/__tests__/tick.test.ts`: `afterEach` now does `TRUNCATE default_routines_v1 CASCADE` and re-INSERTs the heartbeat seed (mirrors store.test.ts). 3. **Fix #4** — `DefaultRoutinesSection.tsx:120`: empty `` now carries `Actions`. 4. **Fix #1** — `tick.ts`: active-hours defer branch detects `row.definitionId !== null` and writes `nextRunAt: null, lastRunAt: adjusted` for default-sourced rows (workspace path unchanged). New regression test added. 5. **Fix #3** — `routes.ts`: all 5 admin handlers (list/get/create/update/destroy) now build `ctx = ctxForActor(actor.id)` AFTER `requireAdmin` and pass that to the routines:*-default bus calls. `adminCtx.userId` changed `'admin'` → `'system'` (only used for the requireAdmin call itself). 6. **Fix #2** — `routes.ts` PUT handler: pre-write check parses `name:` from sourceMd with the same regex as `@ax/skills`, then `routines:get-default` for the URL id, and 400s if names disagree BEFORE calling upsert. Post-call equality check retained as defense in depth (handles URL id doesn't exist, regex missed a multi-line scalar). New test added that uses `upsertDefaultThrow` to prove the upsert was NEVER called when pre-write rejection fires. 7. **Fix #7** — `RoutinesPanel` gained `isAdmin: boolean` prop and gates `` on it; `App.tsx` passes `isAdmin={user.role === 'admin'}` (AuthUser exposes `role`, not `isAdmin`). 8. **Note #1** — `tick.ts` got a comment near `getAgentIds` documenting the `pool.max > 1` dependency. **Verification:** - `pnpm -F @ax/routines test`: 156 passed (was 155 — +1 active-hours-defer test) - `pnpm -F @ax/routines-admin-routes test`: 24 passed (was 23 — +1 pre-write rejection test) - `pnpm -F @ax/channel-web test`: 477 passed (unchanged) - `pnpm -F @ax/preset-k8s test`: 87 passed (unchanged) - `pnpm build`: green - `pnpm lint`: 0 errors, 2 pre-existing warnings **Diff summary:** 8 files, +238/-19 lines. **Skips per plan:** name-based workspace shadowing (HP3), `enabled` column persistence (YAGNI). No plan doc touched. **Files modified:** - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines/src/tick.ts` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines/src/migrations.ts` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines/src/__tests__/tick.test.ts` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines-admin-routes/src/routes.ts` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/routines-admin-routes/src/__tests__/routes.test.ts` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/channel-web/src/App.tsx` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/DefaultRoutinesSection.tsx` - `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/defaults-routines-half/packages/channel-web/src/components/routines/RoutinesPanel.tsx`

input.command: gh pr comment 105 --body "$(cat <<'EOF' Addressed CodeRabbit findings in 62ed4584 (single follow-up commit): **Fixed (7):** - **tick.ts active-hours defer** — default-sourced rows now advance with `nextRunAt: null, lastRunAt: adjusted` so the CHECK constraint doesn't fire when a default routine has activeHours. Regression test added. - **admin-routes PUT id-mismatch** — pre-write parses `name:` from sourceMd and calls `routines:get-default` for the URL `:id`; mismatch returns 400 BEFORE upsert. Post-call equality check kept as belt-and-suspenders. Test asserts upsert was NEVER called when pre-write rejection fires. - **admin handlers actor identity** — all 5 handlers now build `ctx = ctxForActor(actor.id)` after `requireAdmin` so audit logs carry the real actor instead of the static `'admin'` literal. `adminCtx.userId` flipped to `'system'`. - **migration constraint scoping** — `pg_constraint` lookup now filters on `conrelid = 'routines_v1_definitions'::regclass` so an identically-named constraint on another table can't silently skip the ADD. - **tick.test afterEach** — TRUNCATE+re-seed pattern (mirrors store.test.ts) so cross-test mutation of `default_routines_v1` can't leak. - **DefaultRoutinesSection a11y** — actions column gets `Actions`. - **RoutinesPanel admin gate** — `isAdmin: boolean` prop; App.tsx passes `user.role === 'admin'`. Non-admins no longer see the admin-only section. **Documented (1):** - **getAgentIds pool dependency** — comment near the call notes the `pool.max > 1` requirement (production defaults to 10; same condition that already governs `input.fire()` downstream). **Skipped with rationale (2):** - **Workspace-override matched by `name`, not path** — deliberate per plan HP3. Path namespaces are disjoint (`.ax/routines/.md` vs `default:`); sync subscriber already keys workspace rows by `name` via `path = '.ax/routines/.md'`. Not a bug. - **`enabled` column not persisted through upsert** — YAGNI for v1. The admin UI doesn't expose enable/disable; the column has a default and there's no current caller toggling it. Will round-trip when the first caller needs it. Test deltas: routines 155→156, routines-admin-routes 23→24. Build + lint clean. EOF )"
